gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Sequence encoding: `bundle( permute^0(items[0]), permute^1(items[1]), … )`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Each item `i` is permuted by shift `i` to protect its position (a positional encoding),"] # [doc = " then the permuted items are bundled.  The guarantee tag is the meet of `bundle`'s tag"] # [doc = " (model-dependent) and `permute`'s tag (`Exact`), which is just the `bundle` tag — see"] # [doc = " `vsa.md §4` row `encode_seq`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " An empty slice is `Err(EmptyBundle)` — the bundle of zero items is undefined (C1 / G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::EmptyBundle`] — zero items supplied."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NestedBundleUnsupported`] — a MAP-B input is itself a bundle (RR-13)."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] — items have differing lengths."] pub fn encode_seq < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , items : & [& [f64]]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { if items . is_empty () { return Err (VsaError :: EmptyBundle) ; } let permuted : Vec < Vec < f64 > > = items . iter () . enumerate () . map (| (i , item) | model . permute (item , i as i64)) . collect :: < Result < _ , _ > > () ? ; let refs : Vec < & [f64] > = permuted . iter () . map (Vec :: as_slice) . collect () ; model . bundle (& refs) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Set encoding: `bundle(items[0], items[1], …)` — superpose atoms without positional encoding."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The guarantee tag equals the model's `bundle` tag (the permute identity is `Exact` and"] # [doc = " contributes nothing to the meet).  Useful for set/bag membership probes where order does not"] # [doc = " matter."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " An empty slice is `Err(EmptyBundle)` (C1 / G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::EmptyBundle`] — zero items supplied."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NestedBundleUnsupported`] — MAP-B nesting (RR-13)."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] — items have differing lengths."] # [inline] pub fn encode_set < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , items : & [& [f64]]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { if items . is_empty () { return Err (VsaError :: EmptyBundle) ; } model . bundle (items) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One row of the `std.vsa` guarantee matrix (RFC-0016 §4.5 / vsa.md §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Each row covers one `(model_id, op)` pair.  The guarantee tag is read from the authoritative"] # [doc = " RFC-0003 §4 matrix (`mycelium_vsa::matrix_tag`), not invented here (C2 / VR-5)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq)] pub struct OpGuarantee { # [doc = " The VSA model id, e.g. `\"MAP-I\"`."] pub model_id : & 'static str , # [doc = " The operation."] pub op : VsaOp , # [doc = " The guarantee tag (the RFC-0003 §4 matrix entry for this pair)."] pub tag : GuaranteeStrength , # [doc = " Is the result EXPLAIN-able (carries an inspectable artifact such as a bound or trace)?"] # [doc = " `Exact` ops are `false` — they carry no bound (M-I1); all approximate ops are `true`."] pub explain_able : bool , }
gaps.reason: struct `OpGuarantee` uses named fields — no record/product-type surface (only a single-ctor positional shape maps to `type_item`)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `std.vsa` guarantee matrix (vsa.md §4), encoded as data (RFC-0016 §4.5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Rows = `(model, op)` pairs.  Tag = the RFC-0003 §4 normative entry for that pair (copied from"] # [doc = " `mycelium_vsa::RFC0003_MATRIX`; divergence is caught in tests).  `explain_able` = whether"] # [doc = " the op exposes an inspectable bound/trace artifact (C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Notes matching the spec:"] # [doc = " - `bind` is `Exact` for MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC/HRR/FHRR (algebraic; §4.1 erratum) and `Proven` for"] # [doc = "   SBC (the Bloom analysis — `matrix.rs` `(\"SBC\", Bind, Proven)`)."] # [doc = " - `unbind` is `Exact` for MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC (self-inverse); `Empirical` for HRR/FHRR (the"] # [doc = "   residual weak link; §4.1); `Proven` for SBC."] # [doc = " - `bundle` is `Proven` for MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC/SBC and `Empirical` for HRR/FHRR."] # [doc = " - `permute` is `Exact` for **all** models (§4.1 erratum — a fixed coordinate bijection)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG Q3 (SBC bind/bundle `Proven`)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The SBC `bind` and `bundle` cells are `Proven` in the kernel matrix (`matrix.rs`), reflecting"] # [doc = " the Bloom / Counting-Bloom analysis cited in RFC-0003 §4/§5.  This table mirrors that cell"] # [doc = " faithfully.  The exact theorem constants and checked side-conditions are owned by RFC-0003 §5"] # [doc = " and the encoded matrix; they are **cited here, not restated** (VR-5 / vsa.md §7-Q3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG Q3 (HRR/FHRR `bundle` `Empirical`)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " HRR/FHRR `bundle` is `Empirical` (Gaussian/asymptotic).  The sub-Gaussian phasor upgrade to"] # [doc = " `Proven` (Thomas) is **not** pre-claimed here — it requires checked instantiation not yet"] # [doc = " discharged in this crate (VR-5 / vsa.md §7-Q3)."] pub const GUARANTEE_MATRIX : & [OpGuarantee] = & [OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-I" , op : VsaOp :: Bind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-I" , op : VsaOp :: Unbind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-I" , op : VsaOp :: Bundle , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-I" , op : VsaOp :: Permute , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-B" , op : VsaOp :: Bind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-B" , op : VsaOp :: Unbind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-B" , op : VsaOp :: Bundle , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "MAP-B" , op : VsaOp :: Permute , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "BSC" , op : VsaOp :: Bind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "BSC" , op : VsaOp :: Unbind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "BSC" , op : VsaOp :: Bundle , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "BSC" , op : VsaOp :: Permute , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "HRR" , op : VsaOp :: Bind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "HRR" , op : VsaOp :: Unbind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "HRR" , op : VsaOp :: Bundle , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "HRR" , op : VsaOp :: Permute , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "FHRR" , op : VsaOp :: Bind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "FHRR" , op : VsaOp :: Unbind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "FHRR" , op : VsaOp :: Bundle , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "FHRR" , op : VsaOp :: Permute , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "SBC" , op : VsaOp :: Bind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "SBC" , op : VsaOp :: Unbind , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "SBC" , op : VsaOp :: Bundle , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , explain_able : true , } , OpGuarantee { model_id : "SBC" , op : VsaOp :: Permute , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , explain_able : false , } ,] ;

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Look up a row in [`GUARANTEE_MATRIX`] by model id and op.  Returns `None` for an unregistered"] # [doc = " model — an unregistered model has no honest tag to offer (the same convention as"] # [doc = " `mycelium_vsa::matrix_tag`)."] # [must_use] pub fn std_matrix_tag (model_id : & str , op : VsaOp) -> Option < GuaranteeStrength > { GUARANTEE_MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . model_id == model_id && r . op == op) . map (| r | r . tag) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Bind two hypervectors (associate)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC/HRR/FHRR this is an algebraic `Exact` op; for SBC it is `Proven`"] # [doc = " (Bloom analysis — `matrix.rs`).  Both operands must have the same length as the model's"] # [doc = " `dim`; a mismatch is `Err(DimMismatch)`, never a silent coercion (C1 / G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] — the operands' lengths disagree with each other or the model."] # [inline] pub fn bind < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , a : & [f64] , b : & [f64]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . bind (a , b) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Unbind (recover a factor from a bind product)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Exact for self-inverse models (MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC) and SBC (`Proven`); `Empirical` for"] # [doc = " HRR/FHRR (the approximate inverse is lossy — the residual weak link; RFC-0003 §4.1).  For"] # [doc = " HRR/FHRR the result should be cleaned up against a codebook ([`cleanup`]) to obtain a"] # [doc = " usable item — do not treat a raw unbind result as `Exact`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] — the operands' lengths disagree with each other or the model."] # [inline] pub fn unbind < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , c : & [f64] , b : & [f64]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . unbind (c , b) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Bundle (superpose) a non-empty set of hypervectors."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The guarantee tag depends on the model (read from `intrinsic_guarantee(Bundle)`):"] # [doc = " `Proven` for MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC/SBC; `Empirical` for HRR/FHRR.  A bundle over zero items is"] # [doc = " `Err(EmptyBundle)`, never a fabricated zero vector (C1 / G2).  MAP-B nested-bundle inputs"] # [doc = " yield `Err(NestedBundleUnsupported)` — reliability decays `1/2 + 1/2^r` with nesting depth"] # [doc = " `r`, so nesting beyond depth 1 is refused explicitly rather than silently degrading accuracy"] # [doc = " (RR-13)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For a Value-level `Proven` bundle with a checked `CapacityBound` certificate, use the"] # [doc = " Value-level adapters in `mycelium-vsa` directly (e.g. `MapI::bundle_values_certified`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::EmptyBundle`] — zero items supplied."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NestedBundleUnsupported`] — MAP-B input is itself a MAP-B bundle (RR-13)."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] — items have differing lengths."] # [inline] pub fn bundle < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , items : & [& [f64]]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . bundle (items) }

See FLAG Q2 in `vsa.md §7` for the open question about a"] # [doc = " crosstalk-context bound when used decisionally."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `0.0` if either operand has zero norm (the model's documented convention)."] # [inline] pub fn similarity < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , a : & [f64] , b : & [f64]) -> f64 { model . similarity (a , b) }

Same guarantee as"] # [doc = " `bind` for the model (see [`bind`] for the guarantee-tag breakdown)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " See [`bind`]."] # [inline] pub fn bind_role < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , role : & [f64] , filler : & [f64] ,) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { bind (model , role , filler) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Cleanup: nearest-atom indexed retrieval against an item memory."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Empirical` (bounded-lossy item memory, Clarkson Thm 16; M-132).  Returns"] # [doc = " `Match { label, index, confidence, margin }` so the caller can inspect and threshold the"] # [doc = " result — never a silent low-confidence guess (C1/C3 / G2 / FR-C2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Explicit refusals (C1 / G2):"] # [doc = " - `Err(BelowCleanupThreshold)` when `confidence < min_confidence` (the caller-supplied floor)."] # [doc = " - `Err(BelowCleanupThreshold)` when `margin < min_margin` (near-tied slots). See the FLAG in"] # [doc = "   the body: `mycelium-vsa::VsaError` has **no** distinct `Ambiguous` variant, so the"] # [doc = "   margin-shortfall case is surfaced through `BelowCleanupThreshold` (with `confidence` carrying"] # [doc = "   the margin and `threshold` carrying `min_margin`) — never a silent guess. A dedicated variant"] # [doc = "   is a kernel change (FLAG)."] # [doc = " - `Err(EmptyCodebook)` when the memory is empty or the query has the wrong length."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Setting `min_confidence = 0.0` and `min_margin = 0.0` disables the threshold gates,"] # [doc = " returning the raw nearest-neighbour hit (the confidence/margin are still reported in"] # [doc = " `Match` for the caller to inspect)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::EmptyCodebook`] — the memory is empty or query length disagrees with `dim`."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::BelowCleanupThreshold`] — `confidence < min_confidence`, **or** the"] # [doc = "   margin-shortfall case `margin < min_margin` (see the body FLAG: `VsaError` has no"] # [doc = "   `Ambiguous` variant, so both refusals share this variant)."] pub fn cleanup < M : VsaModel > (codebook : & CleanupMemory , query : & [f64] , model : & M , min_confidence : f64 , min_margin : f64 ,) -> Result < Match , VsaError > { let hit = codebook . cleanup (query , model) . ok_or (VsaError :: EmptyCodebook) ? ; if hit . confidence < min_confidence { return Err (VsaError :: BelowCleanupThreshold { confidence : hit . confidence , threshold : min_confidence , }) ; } if hit . margin < min_margin { return Err (VsaError :: BelowCleanupThreshold { confidence : hit . margin , threshold : min_margin , }) ; } Ok (hit) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Compositional reconstruction: unbind `record` by a named `role`, then clean up against"] # [doc = " `memory`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Requires:"] # [doc = " - A `CompositionalReconstruction` manifest (`Err(NotCompositional)` otherwise)."] # [doc = " - The manifest's recipe to name `role` (`Err(UnknownRole)` otherwise)."] # [doc = " - A `Cleanup` decode procedure with a threshold (`Err(NotCompositional)` otherwise)."] # [doc = " - The cleanup confidence to meet the manifest's threshold (`Err(BelowCleanupThreshold)`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`Match`] `{ label, index, confidence, margin }` — the recovered filler atom with its"] # [doc = " confidence/margin so the caller can inspect and judge the quality (C3 / G2 / FR-C2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The guarantee tag is the meet of the model's `unbind` tag and `cleanup`'s `Empirical` tag"] # [doc = " (weakest-wins; RFC-0001 §4.7) — at most `Empirical`, never `Proven`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NotThisModel`] — `record` or `role_atom` is not of the manifest's model."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NotCompositional`] — the manifest does not support compositional reconstruction."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::UnknownRole`] — `role` is not in the manifest's recipe."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::EmptyCodebook`] — `memory` is empty or has the wrong dim."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::BelowCleanupThreshold`] — cleanup confidence is below the manifest threshold."] pub fn reconstruct_role < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , manifest : & ReconInfo , record : & Value , role : & str , role_atom : & Value , memory : & CleanupMemory ,) -> Result < Match , VsaError > { mycelium_vsa :: recon :: reconstruct_role (model , manifest , record , role , role_atom , memory) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Resonator factorization: recover the unknown factor atoms of a bind product."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Opt-in, **probabilistic-only** (`Empirical` ceiling for MAP-I/BSC — never `Proven`; FR-C2)."] # [doc = " For a `Resonator` manifest, reads the iteration budget and decode params, gates on the"] # [doc = " trial-validated [`MAPI_RESONATOR_PROFILE`](mycelium_vsa::MAPI_RESONATOR_PROFILE), and runs"] # [doc = " the resonator loop."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`Factorization`] **only** on a clean `Converged` verdict clearing every per-slot"] # [doc = " confidence/margin threshold.  All non-convergence outcomes are explicit errors carrying the"] # [doc = " inspectable [`ResonatorTrace`](mycelium_vsa::ResonatorTrace) (C3 / G2 / RFC-0009 §5/§6):"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorBudgetExhausted`]"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorOscillating`]"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorStalled`]"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorBelowConfidence`]"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorBelowMargin`]"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG Q4 — `Declared` coverage deferred"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " RFC-0009 §9 Q6 caps resonator factorization at `Empirical` for exact-bind models (MAP-I/BSC)"] # [doc = " and `Declared` for sparse/HRR/FHRR.  This function gates on the MAP-I profile and is the"] # [doc = " validated path; the `Declared` paths for sparse/HRR/FHRR are deferred to RFC-0009 §10.3"] # [doc = " (deferred coverage) and not yet exported in `std.vsa` v0 (vsa.md §7-Q4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NotThisModel`] — `record` is not of the manifest's model."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NotCompositional`] — the manifest is not a `Resonator` decode."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::OutsideEmpiricalProfile`] — the request exceeds the validated regime."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorBudgetExhausted`] — budget reached without convergence."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorOscillating`] — a genuine period-≥2 limit cycle."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorStalled`] — stationary tuple plateaued below `τ_lock`."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorBelowConfidence`] — a slot's confidence is below the threshold."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::ResonatorBelowMargin`] — a slot's margin is below the threshold."] pub fn reconstruct_factors < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , manifest : & ReconInfo , record : & Value , codebooks : & [CleanupMemory] ,) -> Result < Factorization , VsaError > { mycelium_vsa :: recon :: reconstruct_factors (model , manifest , record , codebooks) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment
